author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel, Mark viii. 5, etc., where the miracle of the seven loaves is related.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, Mark viii. 5, etc., where the miracle of the seven loaves is related.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In expounding to you the Holy Scriptures, I as it were break bread for you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do ye in hunger receive it, and break forth with a fulness of phrase from the heart; and ye who are rich in your banquet, be not meagre in good works and deeds.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What I deal out to you is not mine own.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What ye eat, I eat; what ye live upon, I live upon.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We have in heaven a common store-house; for from thence comes the Word of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The “seven loaves” signify the seven-fold operation of the Holy Spirit; the “four thousand men,” the Church established on the four Gospels; “the seven baskets of fragments,” the perfection of the Church.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For by this number very constantly is perfection figured.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For whence is that which is said, “seven times in a day will I praise thee”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Does a man sin who does not praise the Lord so often?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then is “seven times will I praise,” but “I will never cease from praise”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For he who says “seven times,” signifies all time.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whence in this world there are continual revolutions of seven days.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then is “seven times in a day will I praise Thee,” but what is said in another place, “His praise shall always be in my mouth”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: With reference to this perfection, John writes to seven Churches.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Apocalypse is a book of St. John the Evangelist; and he writes “to seven Churches.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Be ye hungered; own ye these baskets.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For those fragments were not lost; but seeing that ye too belong to the Church, they have surely profited you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In that I explain this to you, I minister to Christ; and when ye hear peaceably, ye “sit down.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I in my body sit, but in my heart I am standing, and ministering to you in anxiety; lest peradventure, not the food, but the vessel offend any of you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye know the feast of God, ye have often heard it, that it is for the heart, not for the belly.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Of a truth four thousand men were filled by seven loaves; what is more wonderful than this!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet even this were not enough, had not seven baskets also been filled with the fragments that remained.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: O great mysteries! they were works, and the works spake.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou understand these doings, they are words.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And ye too belong to the four thousand, because ye live under the fourfold Gospel.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To this number the children and women did not belong.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For so it is said, “And they that did eat were four thousand men, excepting women and children.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As though the void of understanding, and the effeminate were without number.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet let even these eat.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let them eat: it may be the children will grow, and will be children no more; it may be the effeminate will be amended, and become chaste.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let them eat; we dispense, we deal out to them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But who these are, God inspecteth His feast, and if they do not amend themselves, He who knew how to invite them thither, knoweth also how to separate them from the rest.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye know it, dearly Beloved; call to mind the parable of the Gospel, how that the Lord came in to inspect the guests at a certain feast of His.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Master of the house who had invited them, as it is written, “found there a man which had not on a wedding garment.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For to the marriage had that Bridegroom invited them who is “fair in beauty above the children of men.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That Bridegroom became deformed because of His deformed spouse, that he might make her fair.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How did the Fair One become deformed?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If I do not prove it, I am blaspheming.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The testimony of his fair beauty the Prophet gives me, who saith, “Thou art fair in beauty above the children of men.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The testimony of his deformity another Prophet gives me, who saith, “We saw Him, and He had no grace, nor beauty; but His countenance was marred, and His whole look deformed.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: O Prophet, who saidst, “Thou art fair in beauty above the children of men;” thou art contradicted; another Prophet cometh out against thee, and saith, “Thou speakest falsely.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We have seen Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is this that thou sayest, ‘Thou art fair in beauty above the children of men?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We have seen Him, and He had no grace nor beauty.’”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Are then these two Prophets at disagreement in the Corner-stone of peace?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Both spake of Christ, both spake of the Cornerstone.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In the corner the walls unite.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If they do not unite, it is not a building, but a ruin.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: No, the Prophets agree, let us not leave them in strife.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yea, rather let us understand their peace; for they know not how to strive.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: O Prophet, who saidst, “Thou art fair in beauty above the children of men;” where didst thou see Him?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Answer me, answer where didst thou see Him?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Being in the form of God, He thought it not robbery to be equal with God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There I saw Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Dost thou doubt that He who is “equal with God” is “fair in beauty above the children of men”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast answered; now let him answer who said, “We saw Him, and He had no grace, nor beauty.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast said so; tell us where didst thou see Him?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He begins from the other’s words; where the other ended, there he begins.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Where did he end?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Who being in the form of God, thought it not robbery to be equal with God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, where he saw Him who was “fair in beauty above the children of men;” do thou tell us, where thou sawest that “He had no grace nor beauty.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But He emptied Himself, taking the form of a servant, being made in the likeness of men, and found in fashion as a man.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Of His deformity he still further says; “He humbled Himself, having become obedient unto death even the death of the cross.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, where I saw Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore are they both in peaceful concord, both are at peace together.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is more “fair” than God?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What more “deformed” than the Crucified?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then this Bridegroom, “fair in beauty above the children of men,” became deformed that He might make His Spouse fair to whom it is said, “O thou beauteous among women,” of whom it is said, “Who is this that cometh up, whitened” with the brightness of light, not the colouring of falsehood!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He then who called them to the wedding, found a man who had not a wedding garment, and He said unto him, “Friend, how camest thou in hither, not having a wedding garment?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And he was speechless.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For he found not what to answer.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And the Master of the house Who had invited him said, “Bind him hands and feet, and cast him into outer darkness; there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For so small a fault, so great a punishment?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For great it is.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is called a small fault not to have “the wedding garment;” small, but only by those who do not understand.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How would He have been so incensed, how would He have so judged, to cast him, on account of the wedding garment which he had not, “bound hands and feet into outer darkness, where was weeping and gnashing of teeth,” unless it had been a very grievous fault, not to have “the wedding garment”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I say this; seeing ye have been invited through me; for though He invited you, He invited you by my ministry.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye are all at the feast, have the wedding garment.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I will explain what it is, that ye may all have it, and if any one now hears me who has it not, let him, before the Master of the house comes and inspects His guests, be changed for the better, let him receive “the wedding garment,” and so sit down in all assurance.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For in truth, dearly Beloved, he who was cast forth from the feast, does not signify one man; far from it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They are many.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And the Lord Himself who put forth this parable, the Bridegroom Himself, who calleth together to the feast, and quickeneth whom He calleth, He hath Himself explained to us, that that man does not denote one man, but many, there, in that very place, in the same parable.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I do not go far for this, I find the explanation there, there I break the bread, and set it before you to be eaten.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He said, when he who had not “the wedding garment was cast out thence into outer darkness,” He said and added immediately, “for many are called, but few chosen.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast cast forth one man from hence, and Thou sayest, “for many are called, but few chosen.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Without doubt the chosen are not cast forth; and they were the few guests who remained; and the “many” were represented in that one, because that one who hath not “the wedding garment” is the body of the wicked.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is “the wedding garment”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us search for it in the Holy Scriptures.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is “the wedding garment”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Without doubt it is something which the bad and good have not in common; let us discover this, and we shall discover “the wedding garment.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Among the gifts of God, what have not the good and bad in common?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That we are men and not beasts, is a gift of God; but this is common to good and bad.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That the light from heaven rises upon us, that the rain descends from the cloud, the fountains flow, the fields yield their fruit; these are gifts, but common to the good and bad.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us go to the marriage feast, let us leave the others without, who being called come not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us consider the guests themselves, that is, Christians.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Baptism is a gift of God, the good and bad have it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Sacraments of the Altar the good and bad receive together.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Saul prophesied for all his wickedness, and in his rage against a holy and most righteous man, even while he was persecuting him, he prophesied.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Are the good only said to believe?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The devils also believe and tremble.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What shall I do?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I have sifted all, and have not yet come to “the wedding garment.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I have unfolded my envelopings, I have considered all, or almost all, and have not yet come to that garment.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Apostle Paul in a certain place has brought me a great collection of excellent things; he has laid them open before me, and I have said to him, “Show me, if so be thou hast found among them that ‘wedding garment.’”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He begins to unfold them one by one, and to say, “Though I speak with the tongues of men and of Angels, though I have all knowledge, and the gift of prophecy, and all faith, so that I could remove mountains; though I distribute all my goods to the poor, and give my body to be burned.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Precious garments! nevertheless, there is not yet here that “wedding garment.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now bring out to us “the wedding garment.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why dost thou keep us in suspense, O Apostle?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Peradventure prophecy is a gift of God which both good and bad have not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “If,” says He, “I have not charity, nothing profiteth me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See “the wedding garment;” put it on, ye guests, that ye may sit down securely.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do not say; “we are too poor to have that garment.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Clothe others, and ye are clothed yourselves.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is winter, clothe the naked.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Christ is naked; and He will give you that “wedding garment” whosoever have it not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Run to Him, beseech Him; He knoweth how to sanctify His faithful ones, He knoweth how to clothe His naked ones.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That ye may be able as having “the wedding garment” to be free from the fear of the outer darkness, and the binding of your members and hands and feet; let not your works fail.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If they fail, with hands bound what canst thou do? with feet bound, whither wilt thou fly?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Keep then that “wedding garment,” put it on, and so sit down in security, when He comes to inspect.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Day of Judgment will come; He is now giving a long space, let him who erewhile was naked now be clothed.
